assistant_message.content:
{
  "finding_id": "openscap_060",
  "rule_id": "Ensure SELinux State is Enforcing",
  "category": "safe_to_remediate",
  "confidence": 0.96,
  "rationale": "Changing SELinux mode to enforcing via /etc/selinux/config and running fixfiles is a standard, low‑risk remediation that does not lock out access; it may require a reboot but will not break core services.",
  "risk_factors": [
    "requires reboot to apply enforcing mode",
    "temporary permissive state may allow policy violations until reboot"
  ],
  "safe_next_steps": [
    "Backup /etc/selinux/config",
    "Set SELINUX=enforcing in /etc/selinux/config",
    "Run fixfiles onboot to restore labels",
    "Reboot the system"
  ],
  "requires_reboot": true,
  "touches_authn_authz": false,
  "touches_networking": false,
  "touches_filesystems": false
}
